mnemonics: N(ε)bd: Neighborhoods are defined by an Epsilon (ε) radius ball around a point.
keyPoints: A neighborhood of a point x is a set that contains an open ball centered at x.
The open ball B(x; ε) = { y in X | d(x,y) < ε } is the fundamental neighborhood.
Neighborhoods are the building blocks for defining open sets, closed sets, and limits.
commonMistakes: Assuming any set containing a point is its neighborhood (it must contain an OPEN ball).
Confusing the neighborhood's size (ε) with the point itself; different ε values create different neighborhoods for the same point.
